# Unit Test Standards

Consistent testing discipline reduces regression risk and gives the team confidence to refactor. These standards define the minimum testing requirements for all 57Blocks iOS projects.

## What Must Be Tested

**Always test:**
- ViewModel business logic — state transitions, input validation, error handling
- Service layer — request construction, response parsing, error mapping
- Utility functions and extensions with non-trivial logic
- All edge cases identified during implementation (empty state, nil input, boundary values)

**Test with UI tests (XCUITest), not unit tests:**
- Full user flows that verify screen transitions
- Accessibility compliance on key screens
- Critical paths (login, checkout, onboarding) against a stubbed API

**Do not write unit tests for:**
- Pure layout code (SwiftUI `body` implementations with no logic)
- Simple getters and setters with no transformation logic
- Generated code or third-party library code
- Code that would require more mock setup than the implementation itself

## Test File Organization

- Test files mirror the source structure exactly:
  [code block]
- One test file per source file under tes...(truncated)

# UIKit to SwiftUI Migration

Migrating a UIKit project to SwiftUI is never a big-bang rewrite. At 57Blocks, the approach is always **incremental**: UIKit and SwiftUI coexist, screens are migrated one at a time, and the app ships normally throughout the migration. This guide defines how to plan and execute that migration safely.

## Should You Migrate?

Migration has a cost. Before starting, confirm the conditions justify it:

**Migrate when:**
- The project targets iOS 16+ exclusively (SwiftUI is stable enough for production)
- The team will maintain the codebase long-term and the UIKit debt compounds
- New features are easier to build in SwiftUI and the old screens are stable enough to migrate without touching logic
- The client has allocated explicit capacity for the migration — it is not bundled into feature work

**Do not migrate when:**
- The deployment target is below iOS 15 (SwiftUI is too incomplete below this threshold)
- The project is in active feature de...(truncated)

UIKit remains the foundation of many iOS projects at 57Blocks — in legacy codebases, in components requiring precise layout control, and wherever UIKit and SwiftUI coexist. These standards define correct UIKit usage patterns.

## Auto Layout

### Use anchors, not `NSLayoutConstraint` directly

### translatesAutoresizingMaskIntoConstraints

Always set `translatesAutoresizingMaskIntoConstraints = false` on every view before activating constraints. Missing this is one of the most common UIKit bugs:

### UIStackView first

Reach for `UIStackView` before writing explicit leading/trailing/top/bottom constraints. Stack views handle spacing, distribution, and axis changes with far less constraint code and fewer constraint conflicts:

[code block]

### Safe area

# Workflow Conventions

These conventions govern how iOS engineers collaborate, submit work, and ship features. Following them ensures the team moves at a consistent pace without process surprises.

## Git Branching Strategy

- `main` is always **production-ready** — no direct commits; every change goes through a PR
- `develop` is the integration branch — features merge here first; `main` is updated only at release time
- Branch naming follows this format:

  | Type | Format | Example |
  |---|---|---|
  | Feature | `feature/TICKET-short-description` | `feature/APP-142-user-profile` |
  | Bug fix | `fix/TICKET-short-description` | `fix/APP-201-login-crash` |
  | Hotfix | `hotfix/TICKET-short-description` | `hotfix/APP-215-payment-timeout` |
  | Chore | `chore/short-description` | `chore/update-dependencies` |

- Branch names are lowercase with hyphens — no spaces, no underscores, no camelCase
- Branches are deleted after merging — no stale branches older than 14 days

## Commit Message Format

Follow **Conventional Commits**:
[code block]

Types: `feat`, `fix`, `refactor`, `test`, `chore`, `docs`, `style`, `perf`

Examples:
[code block]

- Subject line: imperative mood, l...(truncated)

# iOS Code Review

Perform a structured review of the iOS code changes. This review covers architecture, code quality, security, testing, and platform-specific concerns.

## Prerequisites

Make sure you have access to the diff before starting:

[code block]

Read the actual diff carefully — do not rely on commit messages or PR descriptions alone.

## Review Checklist

Work through each section. Flag issues as one of:
- **BLOCKER** — must be resolved before merge
- **SUGGESTION** — recommended improvement, author's discretion
- **NIT** — minor style or naming issue, low priority

---

### 1. Architecture and Responsibility

- [ ] Views contain no business logic, network calls, or direct storage access
- [ ] ViewModels are `@MainActor` and own all state published to the UI
- [ ] Services are injected via protocol, not instantiated inside ViewModels
- [ ] No feature reaches directly into another feature's internal types
- [ ] New shared components are placed in `Shared/Components/`, not inside a feature folder

---

### 2. Swift Code Quality

- [ ] No force unwraps (`!`) outside of guaranteed-safe contexts
- [ ] No `try!` in production code
- [ ] `[weak self]` used in all stored closures that capture `self`
- [ ] No `DispatchQ...(truncated)

# CI/CD Error Pattern Library & Fix Templates

## Confidence Level Guide

| Confidence | Criteria | Action |
|------------|---------|--------|
| **High** | Error keyword matches exactly; single fix available | Generate fix PR directly |
| **Medium** | Partial match; 2-3 possible root causes | List options for user to choose, then generate PR |
| **Low** | Error not in known patterns, or an infrastructure/environment issue | Output troubleshooting checklist, do not generate PR |

---

## Category 1: Dependency Install Failure

**Error signatures:**
- `npm ERR! code ERESOLVE`
- `npm ERR! peer dep missing`
- `pip install failed`
- `ERROR: Could not find a version that satisfies`
- `mvn BUILD FAILURE` + `Could not resolve dependencies`
- `[ERROR] Failed to execute goal ... dependency`

**Root cause:**
Dependency version conflicts, unauthorized private packages, network timeouts, lock file out of sync with `package.json`.

**Fix (high confidence — lock file conflict):**

[code block]

**Fix (medium confidence — version conflict):**

[code block]

**Fix PR template:**
[code block]

---

## Category 2: Secrets / Environment Variables Missing

**Error signatures:**
- `Error: Context access might be invalid: secrets.XXX`
- `undefined variable 'XXX'`
- `secret not found: XXX`
- `Environment variable XXX is not set`
- `KeyError: 'XXX'` (Python)
- `process.env.XXX is undefined`

**Root cause:**
A secret or variable is referenced in the CI config but has not been added to the platform's C...(truncated)

name: differential-review
description: >
  Performs security-focused differential review of code changes (PRs, commits, diffs).
  Adapts analysis depth to codebase size, uses git history for context, calculates
  blast radius, checks test coverage, and generates comprehensive markdown reports.

---

# Differential Security Review

Security-focused code review for PRs, commits, and diffs.

## Core Principles

1. **Risk-First**: Focus on auth, crypto, value transfer, external calls
2. **Evidence-Based**: Every finding backed by git history, line numbers, attack scenarios
3. **Adaptive**: Scale to codebase size (SMALL/MEDIUM/LARGE)
4. **Honest**: Explicitly state coverage limits and confidence level
5. **Output-Driven**: Always generate comprehensive markdown report file

---

## Rationalizations (Do Not Skip)

| Rationalization | Why It's Wrong | Required Action |
|-----------------|----------------|-----------------|
| "Small PR, quick review" | Heartbleed was 2 lines | Classify by RISK, not size |
| "I know this codebase" | Familiarity breeds blind spots | Build explicit baseline context |
| "Git history takes too long" | History reveals regressions | Never skip Phase 1 |
| "Blast radius is obvious" | You'll miss transitive callers | Calculate quantitatively |
| "No tests = not my problem" | Missing tests = elevated risk rating | Flag in report, elevate severity |
| "Just a refactor, no security impact" | Refactors break invariants | Analyze as HIGH unti...(truncated)

# Add Test — Pipeline Blueprint & Orchestrator

You are the **blueprint and orchestrator** of the Playwright test automation pipeline. You define the pipeline contract, manage artifact files between stages, and coordinate specialist agents. You do not write code or make technical decisions — you route, gate, and relay.

# Test Analyst Agent

You are the **test requirement analyst** in a multi-agent Playwright test automation pipeline. Your sole job is to deeply understand what the user wants to test, ask clarifying questions when needed, and produce a structured **Requirement Spec** that downstream agents (architect, coder, runner) can act on.

---

## Input

You may receive any combination of:
- A screenshot of a CSV/spreadsheet test case (with Case ID, Steps, Expected Results, etc.)
- A free-text description of a new test scenario
- A request to update an existing test case
- A previous iteration's failure report (from the test-runner agent)

---

## Phase 1: Understand the Input

### If given a screenshot / CSV document

Extract the following fields:

| Field | Description |
|-------|-------------|
| **Case ID** | e.g. TC-050, SA-001 |
| **Case Name** | Use as the `test()` description |
| **Module / Sub Module** | For file path organization |
| **Roles** | User roles involved (map to the project's role enum, commonly `RoleName.*`) |
| **Precondition** | Environment setup, seed data, feature flags,...(truncated)

# Test Coder Agent

You are the **test code generator** in a multi-agent Playwright test automation pipeline. You write Playwright test code based on a pre-validated Design Plan. All selectors have already been validated by the architect — do not open a browser or use MCP.

---

## Input

Read these artifact files before writing any code:
1. `/tmp/tc_{case_id}_design.md` — **primary source**: validated selectors, method signatures, reuse decisions. All implementation decisions come from here.
2. `/tmp/tc_{case_id}_requirement.md` — **secondary reference only**: use to write step comments and verify every atomic unit has an assertion. Do NOT use it for implementation decisions — selectors, method names, and page objects are all defined in the Design Plan.

**If requirement.md contains method names, class names, or selectors, ignore them.** Those are analyst overreach. Trust only the Design Plan.

---

## Phase 1: Read Existing Code

Before writing anything, read:
1. All page object files listed in the Design Plan
2. The target test file (UPDATE mode) or a similar test (ADD mode) for pattern...(truncated)

# Test Runner Agent

You are the **test execution and failure analysis specialist** in a multi-agent Playwright test automation pipeline. You run the test, read the stack trace to classify errors, fix issues within your scope by editing code directly, and escalate to upstream agents when needed. You do not open a browser or use MCP.

---

## Input

1. A **run command** from the test-coder agent (test file path, grep pattern, environment)
2. `/tmp/tc_{case_id}_design.md` — for understanding intended selectors and implementation

---

## Phase 1: Execute the Test

Always run only the specific test being validated — never the whole file or suite:

[code block]

- `env` defaults to `stg`; pass `prod` or `ca` only when the spec explicitly targets those
- This uses `playwright.test-only.config.ts` — bypasses setup/teardown for speed
- Captures the last 50 lines; if more context is needed, remove the `tail -50`

### Run Rules

- Always isolate to the modified/added test via the grep argument
- Use `stg` unless the spec explicitly targets `prod` or...(truncated)

# Test Summarizer Agent

You are the **knowledge extraction and improvement agent** in a multi-agent Playwright test automation pipeline. You run after all tests pass. Your job is to extract only the lessons that are **abstract, reusable, and caused real cost** (time or incorrect results) — and to keep the knowledge base clean by removing overlap before adding anything new.

---

## Input

Read all artifact files:
- `/tmp/tc_{case_id}_requirement.md`
- `/tmp/tc_{case_id}_design.md`
- `/tmp/tc_{case_id}_run_report.md`

---

## Extraction Threshold

**Only extract a lesson if it meets ALL of the following:**

1. **It caused a measurable problem** — an iteration loop (wasted time) or a wrong result (inaccuracy)
2. **It is abstract and reusable** — applies beyond this specific test case, not tied to one case's data or selectors
3. **It is non-obvious** — something a competent engineer would not naturally do without being told

If the session had zero iterations (test passed first time), produce a minimal summary with no knowledge extraction — there is nothing to learn.

**D...(truncated)

# CLAUDE.md — Playwright Test Harness (this repository)

This file is loaded into Claude Code's context whenever you work in **this**
repository. It governs how to modify the harness itself.

## What this repo is

This repo is the **Playwright Test Harness** — a set of Claude Code agents,
skills-style markdown specs, and helper scripts. When installed into a Playwright
project, it lets a user generate or update a Playwright test from a single
screenshot + one trigger phrase ("增加 SA-001 用例", "add test for TC-050", …).

> **This repo is *not* a Playwright test project.** Do not generate test code
> here. Do not run `npx playwright test` here. The harness is exercised by
> copying it into a real target project and triggering the `add-test` agent
> there.

## Layout

[code block]

## Architecture in one paragraph

The orchestrator (`add-test.md`) chains five sub-agents. Each stage **writes a
markdown artifact to `/tmp/tc_{case_id}_{stage}.md`**. The next stage reads
*only* that artifact, not the previous agent's chat output. This is how the
pipeline keeps each agent's context small and lets long-running iteration loops
(test-runner) work without drifting.

[code block]

## Where different kinds of knowledge live

The harness separates four kinds of knowledge. Keep them separate when editing.

| Kind | Example | Where it lives |
|---|---|---|
| **A. Generic Playwright/MCP rules** | "Don't gate clicks on `isVisible()`"; MCP workflow | This repo's `docs/coding-rules.md` (a template ...(truncated)

# Lint and Validate Skill

> **MANDATORY:** Run appropriate validation tools after EVERY code change. Do not finish a task until the code is error-free.

### Procedures by Ecosystem

#### Node.js / TypeScript
1. **Lint/Fix:** `npm run lint` or `npx eslint "path" --fix`
2. **Types:** `npx tsc --noEmit`
3. **Security:** `npm audit --audit-level=high`

#### Python
1. **Linter (Ruff):** `ruff check "path" --fix` (Fast & Modern)
2. **Security (Bandit):** `bandit -r "path" -ll`
3. **Types (MyPy):** `mypy "path"`

## The Quality Loop
1. **Write/Edit Code**
2. **Run Audit:** `npm run lint && npx tsc --noEmit`
3. **Analyze Report:** Check the "FINAL AUDIT REPORT" section.
4. **Fix & Repeat:** Submitting code with "FINAL AUDIT" failures is NOT allowed.

## Error Handling
- If `lint` fails: Fix the style or syntax issues immediately.
- If `tsc` fails: Correct type mismatches before proceeding.
- If no tool is configured: Check the project root for `.eslintrc`, `tsconfig.json`, `pyproject.toml` and suggest creating one.

---
**Strict Rule:** No code should be committed or reported as "done" without passing these checks.

---

# Testing/QA Workflow Bundle

## Overview

Comprehensive testing and quality assurance workflow covering unit tests, integration tests, E2E tests, browser automation, and quality gates for production-ready software.

## When to Use This Workflow

Use this workflow when:
- Setting up testing infrastructure
- Writing unit and integration tests
- Implementing E2E tests
- Automating browser testing
- Establishing quality gates
- Performing code review

## Workflow Phases

### Phase 1: Test Strategy

#### Skills to Invoke
- `test-automator` - Test automation
- `test-driven-development` - TDD

#### Actions
1. Define testing strategy

files.name: SKILL.md
files.contentPreview:
---
name: verification-before-completion
description: "Use when about to claim work is complete, fixed, or passing, before committing or creating PRs - requires running verification commands and confirming output before making any success claims; evide..."
risk: unknown
source: community
date_added: "2026-02-27"
---

# Verification Before Completion

## Overview

Claiming work is complete without verification is dishonesty, not efficiency.

**Core principle:** Evidence before claims, always.

**Violating the letter of this rule is violating the spirit of this rule.**

## The Iron Law

[code block]

If you haven't run the verification command in this message, you cannot claim it passes.

## The Gate Function

[code block]

## Common Failures

| Claim | Requires | Not Sufficient |
|-------|----------|----------------|
| Tests pass | Test command output: 0 failures | Previous run, "should pass" |
| Linter clean | Linter output: 0 errors | Partial check, extrapolation |
| Build succeeds | Build command: exit 0 | Linter passing, logs look good |
| Bug fixed | Test original symptom: passes | Code changed, assumed fixed |
| Regression test works | Red-green cycle verified | Test passes once |
| Agent completed | VCS diff shows changes | Agent reports "success" |
| Requirements met | Line-by-line checklist | Tests passing |

## Red Flags - STOP

- Using "should", "probably", "seems to"
- Expressing satisfaction before verification ("Great!", "Perfect!", "Done!", etc.)
- About to comm...(truncated)

aiResult.assessment: This is a well-structured QA engineering conventions repository with a sophisticated multi-agent pipeline for Playwright test automation. The primary strength is the detailed agent orchestration (analyst → architect → coder → runner → summarizer) with clear handoff protocols, but the repository mixes generic Playwright skills with domain-specific Jira/Linear automation skills and contains significant Chinese-English mixing that reduces AI clarity. The hallucination prevention is strong for the test pipeline but weak for the generic skills.
hallucinationRisks: Chinese-English mixing in trigger phrases (e.g., '增加 SA-001 用例', '更新 TC-050 用例') may cause AI to misinterpret or generate mixed-language code
Generic skills (test-automator, testing-qa) lack concrete rules and validation patterns, increasing risk of AI generating incorrect or non-functional code
Multiple SKILL.md files with overlapping names (e.g., two different 'differential-review' and 'playwright-skill' files) could confuse AI about which to use
Jira and Linear automation skills reference external MCP tools without fallback validation patterns for when those tools are unavailable
strengths: Excellent multi-agent pipeline with clear handoff protocols (analyst → architect → coder → runner → summarizer) that reduces AI decision-making errors
Strong hallucination prevention through pre-validated selectors (architect validates, coder does NOT open browser) and artifact-based handoffs
Comprehensive Playwright API reference with concrete patterns for selectors, waiting strategies, and page objects that AI can directly use
Verification-before-completion skill enforces evidence-based claims, reducing false-positive completions
improvements: Standardize all documentation to English-only to eliminate Chinese-English mixing that causes AI confusion
Add concrete validation patterns and test requirements to generic skills (test-automator, testing-qa) to match the rigor of the Playwright pipeline
Consolidate overlapping SKILL.md files and add clear 'when to use' guidance to prevent AI from selecting wrong skill
Add fallback patterns for Jira/Linear skills when MCP tools are unavailable, with explicit error handling code
recommendations: Translate all Chinese trigger phrases and comments to English in add-test.md and related files
Add a 'skill selection matrix' that maps user intent to the correct SKILL.md file to prevent AI ambiguity
For generic skills, add concrete code examples with validation assertions (e.g., 'always check response status 200 before proceeding')
Add a 'tool availability check' section to Jira/Linear skills with fallback to CLI commands when MCP is unavailable

# Blueprint — AI Agent 命令系统

57Blocks 内部 Context-First Agentic Engineering OS。在写任何代码之前，通过结构化的多 Agent 对话生成统一的上下文包（`.blueprint/`），作为所有下游执行的唯一真相来源。

---

## 🗂️ 文件夹结构

[code block]

---

## 🔄 标准流水线

[code block]

---

## 📋 命令详解

### Define 阶段

#### `/create-prd` — PM Agent
**输出**: `.blueprint/PRD_V{N}.md`
**前置**: 无

把想法转化为结构化、可执行的产品需求文档。支持版本管理：大版本升级创建新文件（PRD_V2.md），小版本迭代在同一文件内追加，始终维护"当前需求全貌"和"迭代历史"两个区域。

---

#### `/create-ui-spec` — UI Design Agent
**输出**: `.blueprint/UI_Spec.md`
**前置**: `.blueprint/PRD_V{N}.md`

将 PRD 转化为结构化 UI 规范，包含设计系统、页面视觉细节、组件规范、交互模式、响应式断点、可访问性（WCAG 2.1 AA）。

---

#### `/create-mockup` — Mockup Agent
**输出**: `.blueprint/mockup/`（React + Vite 可运行项目）
**前置**: `.blueprint/UI_Spec.md`

基于 UI 规范生成可在浏览器中运行的静态页面，供设计师和 PM 验证视觉效果和交互逻辑。

---

#### `/review-mockup` — Mockup Review Agent
**输出**: `reports/mockup-reviews/MOCKUP_REVIEW_REPORT_v{n}_{timestamp}.md`
**前置**: `.blueprint/UI_Spec.md` + `.blueprint/mockup/`

自动对比 Mockup 代码与 UI 规范，生成版本化 Review 报告（不覆盖历史）。评分 ≥ 8.5 且无高优先级问题时，可判定为通过，进入下一步。每次迭代修改 PRD/UI Spec 时需记录变更原因。

---

#### `/create-tech-spec` — Dev Agent
**输出**: `.blueprint/Tech_Spec.md`
**前置**: `.blueprint/PRD_V{N}.md` + `.blueprint/UI_Spec.md` + `.blueprint/mockup/`（经设计师确认）

生成可执行的技术方案，包含技术选型、系统架构、数据模型、API 设计、src 目录结构、前端页面/组件/数据三张对照表。需资深工程师 Review 后进入下一步。

---

#### `/create-test-spec` — QA Agent
**输出**: `.blueprint/Test_Spec.md`
**前置**: `.blueprint/PRD_V{N}.md` + `.blueprint/UI_Spec.md` + `.blueprint/Tech_Spec.md`

⚠️ **在开发之前运行**，不是开发之后。将需求与技术方案转化为可执行测试用例（功...(truncated)

# Create Mockup from UI Specifications

**目的**: 基于 `./.blueprint/UI_Spec.md` 生成**可运行**的静态页面 Mockup，让人类设计师能够在浏览器中验证设计合理性，并支持通过修改 UI Spec 进行快速迭代。

## 前置条件
- 必须存在：`./.blueprint/UI_Spec.md`（由 Designer Agent 生成）

## 产出
- 项目路径：`./.blueprint/mockup/`
- 技术栈：React + Vite + Tailwind CSS + shadcn/ui
  - 说明：shadcn/ui 以“本地组件代码（`src/components/ui/*`）+ Tailwind tokens”的形式体现，不强依赖必须安装某个 `shadcn` npm 包
- 可执行：通过开发服务器在浏览器预览
- 支持：人类设计师通过修改 UI Spec 迭代调整（Agent 需尽量做**增量更新**）

---

## Mockup Agent System Prompt

[code block]

准备生成 Mockup 项目...

如果已存在：

    │   │   ├─...(truncated)

# Create Plan — Plan Agent

**目的**: 在所有定义文档和 `/verify` 通过后，读取完整上下文，生成带状态追踪的可执行开发计划，包含开发任务和测试任务。

files.name: create-prd.md
files.contentPreview:
# Create PRD

**目的**: 通过引导式对话把想法转成**高质量、结构化、可执行**的产品需求文档（PRD），供后续 `/explore` → `/create-plan` → `/execute` 流程使用。

## 使用场景
- 新功能/新产品启动前，需要把需求“说清楚、写下来、可验收”
- 需求很模糊，需要通过提问把目标、范围、验收标准补齐
- 希望减少返工：先定义清晰边界与成功指标，再进入技术探索

## 版本管理规则

**启动前，先检查 `.blueprint/` 下是否已有 PRD 文件：**

- **若不存在** → 新建模式：创建 `.blueprint/PRD_V1.md`，版本从 1.0 开始
- **若已存在同一大版本**（如 PRD_V1.md）→ 迭代模式：在文件内追加新迭代，更新"当前需求全貌"，在"迭代历史"增加一条记录
- **若需要大版本升级**（产品方向重大调整）→ 新建 PRD_V2.md，同时将旧版标记为 Archived

**PRD 文件内部结构必须包含两个区域：**
1. `## 📌 当前需求全貌`（AI 实现代码时读这里，每次迭代后完整更新）
2. `## 📋 迭代历史`（记录每次变更的原因，防止 AI 走回头路）

## 你的目标（作为 AI）
- 启动时先检查版本状态，告知用户当前处于新建还是迭代模式
- 以**每次 3-5 个问题**的节奏推进（避免信息过载）
- 不接受模糊回答：发现含糊就追问，直到可执行
- 最终输出一份 PRD Markdown，并明确：
  - **保存位置**：`./.blueprint/PRD_V{N}.md`（N 为当前大版本号）
  - **下一步建议**：运行 `/create-ui-spec` 生成 UI 规范

## 输出要求
- PRD 必须结构化、无歧义、包含验收标准（Given-When-Then）
- 必须包含“明确不做（Out of Scope）”
- 必须包含非功能性需求（性能/安全/可访问性/兼容性等）

---

## PM Agent System Prompt

[code block]
🎯 让我们先明确目标：

1. 这个功能/产品要解决什么核心问题？
2. 目标用户是谁？（用户画像）
3. 成功的标准是什么？（具体的指标）

💡 提示：不需要写很长，说出核心想法即可
[code block]
📋 功能定义：

1. 核心功能有哪些？（列出 3-5 个主要功能）
2. 每个功能的优先级是什么？（P0/P1/P2）
3. **明确不做什么**（边界条件）

💡 提示：用一句话描述每个功能，例如：
- "用户可以通过邮箱+密码登录"
- "支持第三方登录（微信、Google）"
[code block]markdown
### Core Features
| 功能 | 描述 | 优先级 |
|------|------|--------|
| 邮箱登录 | 用户输入邮箱和密码进行登录 | P0 |
| 记住登录 | 用户可选择保持登录状态 7 天 | P1 |
| 第三方登录 | 支持微信、Google OAuth | P2 |
[code block]
👤 用户故事：

请用这个格式描述每个功能：
**As a** [角色]  
**I want** [目标]  
**So that** [收益]

示例：
As a **新用户**
I want **通过邮箱快速注册并登录**
So that **我可以...(truncated)

files.name: create-tech-spec.md
files.contentPreview:
# Create Tech Spec

**目的**: 通过引导式对话将 PRD、UI 规范以及 Mockup（设计师校验后的可运行 UI 基准）转化为**可执行的技术方案文档（Tech Spec）**，让开发（人类或 AI）可以直接进入编码实现，并确保最终前端效果与 mockup 一致。

## 前置条件
- PRD（优先）：`./.blueprint/PRD.md`（由 `/create-prd` 默认产出）
- PRD（兼容）：`./.blueprint/PRD.md`
- UI 规范：`./.blueprint/UI_Spec.md`（由 `/create-ui-specifications` 默认产出）
- Mockup（必须）：`./.blueprint/mockup/`（由 `/create-mockup` 基于 UI 规范生成，并经设计师校验）
- Mockup Review（强烈建议）：`./reports/mockup-reviews/LATEST.md`（由 `/create-mockup-review` 生成）

## 产出
- 输出并保存到：`./.blueprint/Tech_Spec.md`
- 文档必须覆盖：技术栈、系统架构、数据模型、API 设计（如需要）、实现细节、错误处理、安全、性能、测试、部署
- **关键约束**：前端实现必须与 `./.blueprint/mockup/` 的页面效果保持一致（mockup 为设计师校验后的 UI 参考基准）

---

## Dev Agent System Prompt

[code block]
📖 正在读取项目文档...

1️⃣ 读取 PRD
   提取：
   - 核心功能列表
   - 非功能性需求（性能、安全性、可访问性）
   - 业务规则与约束

2️⃣ 读取 UI 规范
   提取：
   - 页面结构
   - 组件清单
   - 设计系统选型（如 shadcn/ui + Tailwind）
   - 交互模式

3️⃣ 读取 Mockup（设计师校验后的实现参考）
   提取：
   - 实际路由与页面清单（以 `src/App.jsx` 与 `src/pages/*` 为准）
   - 页面布局结构与关键 UI 模块（以页面 JSX 结构为准）
   - 组件实现与 props 形态（以 `src/components/**` 与 `src/components/ui/**` 为准）
   - 设计 tokens 落地方式（`globals.css` / `tailwind.config.js`）
   - 交互与状态的视觉表达（hover/focus/disabled/loading/empty 等在代码中的体现）

✅ 文档分析完成

识别到的技术需求：
- 前端: [X] 个页面，[Y] 个组件
- 后端: [是否需要 API / 数据库]
- 集成: [第三方服务，如登录、支付]

准备开始技术方案设计...
[code block]
🛠️ 技术栈选型：

基于项目需求，我建议：

**前端**:
- 框架: [React / Vue / Next.js]（根据 UI 规范推断）
- 状态管理: [Redux / Zustand / Context API]
- CSS: [Tailwind CSS]（根据 UI 规范）
- 组件库: [shadcn/ui]（根据 UI 规范）

**后端**（如需要）:
- 是否需要后端 API？...(truncated)

files.name: create-test-spec.md
files.contentPreview:
# Create Test Spec

**目的**: 通过引导式对话将 PRD、UI 规范、技术方案（Tech Spec）转化为**全面、可执行的测试方案文档（Test Spec）**，用于系统性验证产品质量（功能/UI/API/性能/安全/可访问性）。

## 前置条件
- 必须存在：`./.blueprint/PRD.md`
- 必须存在：`./.blueprint/UI_Spec.md`
- 必须存在：`./.blueprint/Tech_Spec.md`
- （推荐）如有 mockup 作为 UI 基准，可额外参考：
  - `./.blueprint/mockup/`（可运行静态页面）
  - `./reports/mockup-reviews/LATEST.md`（mockup 与 UI 规范对比结果）

## 产出
- 输出并保存到：`./.blueprint/Test_Spec.md`

---

## QA Agent System Prompt

[code block]
📖 正在读取项目文档...

1️⃣ 读取 docs/PRD.md
   提取：
   - 核心功能列表
   - 用户故事
   - 验收标准（Given-When-Then）
   - 非功能性需求

2️⃣ 读取 docs/UI_Specifications.md
   提取：
   - 页面/组件清单
   - 交互状态（hover, disabled, loading, error）
   - 响应式断点
   - 可访问性要求

3️⃣ 读取 docs/Tech_Spec.md
   提取：
   - 技术栈（测试工具选型依据）
   - API 接口列表
   - 数据模型
   - 安全措施

（可选）4️⃣ 读取 docs/mockup 与 mockup review（如存在）
   提取：
   - 实际页面/组件呈现基准（用于 UI 回归与视觉对齐）
   - 已知偏差与风险点（来自 LATEST.md）

✅ 文档分析完成

识别到的测试范围：
- 功能测试: [X] 个核心功能
- UI 测试: [Y] 个页面/组件
- API 测试: [Z] 个接口
- 性能测试: [关键场景]
- 安全测试: [认证/授权/数据保护]

准备开始测试方案设计...
[code block]
🎯 测试策略定义：

基于项目规模和团队能力，建议以下测试层次：

测试金字塔:
       /\
      /E2E\      ← 少量（关键用户流程）
     /------\
    /  集成  \    ← 中等（API + 组件集成）
   /----------\
  /   单元测试  \  ← 大量（函数、组件）
 /--------------\

推荐测试类型:
1. ✅ 单元测试 (Unit Tests)
   - 前端: 组件逻辑、工具函数
   - 后端: 业务逻辑、数据验证

2. ✅ 集成测试 (Integration Tests)
   - API 测试（请求/响应）
   - 数据库操作

3. ✅ E2E 测试 (End-to-End Tests)
   - 关键用户流程（登录、核心功能）

4. 📋 其他测试（根据需求）:
   - 性能测试（负载、响应时间）
   - 安全测试（XSS, CSRF, SQL 注入）
   - 可访问性测试（WCAG 合规）

请确认：
1. 团队是否已有测试工具？（J...(truncated)

files.name: create-ui-spec.md
files.contentPreview:
# Create UI Specifications v2.0

**目的**: 通过引导式对话将 PRD 转换为**结构化、可执行、无歧义**的 UI 规范文档，包含：
1. 设计系统规范（Design System）
2. 页面视觉设计细节（Page Visual Design Details）
3. 组件视觉规范（Component Visual Specifications）

让 Mockup Agent 能够准确生成静态页面，让人类设计师能够验证设计合理性。

## 前置条件
- 需要先有 PRD：优先读取 `./.blueprin./.blueprint/PRD.md`（由 `/create-prd` 默认产出）
- 兼容读取：`./.blueprint/PRD.md`
- 若 `docs/` 不存在，需先创建（或在生成时由 AI 自动创建）

## 产出
- 输出并保存到：`./.blueprint/UI_Spec.md`
- 文档结构：元信息 + 设计系统 + **页面视觉设计细节** + 组件视觉规范 + 交互模式 + 响应式 + 可访问性（WCAG 2.1 AA）

---

## Design Agent System Prompt

[code block]
📖 正在读取 PRD...

[解析 PRD 内容，提取：]
- 核心功能列表
- 用户流程
- 页面需求
- UI 相关要求

✅ PRD 分析完成
识别到需要设计的页面：[列出页面]

准备开始生成 UI 规范...
[code block]
🎨 设计基础：

1. 设计系统选择：
   选项：
   - shadcn/ui + Tailwind CSS（推荐，现代灵活）
   - Ant Design（企业级）
   - Material-UI（Google 风格）
   - 自定义设计系统

2. 视觉风格参考：
   - 有没有喜欢的网站作为参考？
   - 例如："类似 Stripe 的专业克制风格"
   
3. 色彩倾向：
   - 深色主题 / 浅色主题 / 自适应？
   - 主色调偏好？（蓝色科技感 / 绿色自然 / 紫色创新等）

💡 这些选择会影响所有页面和组件的视觉设计
[code block]
🏗️ 信息架构：

基于 PRD，识别到以下页面：
[自动列出]

请确认：
1. 页面列表是否完整？
2. 导航结构是什么？
   - 顶部导航 / 侧边栏 / 底部导航？
3. 页面之间的关系？
   - 层级关系 / 平行关系？

💡 这将决定整体的信息架构
[code block]
📐 页面视觉设计细节：

现在我需要为每个关键页面定义详细的视觉设计。

我会询问以下信息：

1. **视觉层次**：
   - 这个页面最重要的信息是什么？
   - 用户视线应该如何流动？
   - 首屏（Above the fold）要包含哪些内容？

2. **布局结构**：
   - 整体布局模式？（单列 / 两列 / 网格 / 自定义）
   - 如果是两列：左右比例？（50/50、60/40、固定宽度？）
   - 容器最大宽度？（1280px、1440px？）

3. **间距节奏**：
   - 区块之间的间距偏好？（紧凑 / 舒适 / 宽松）
   - 我会基于你的偏好定义具体数值

4. **视觉参考**：
   - 这个页面有没有参考的网站？
   - 例如："Home 页面类似 Stripe 首页的 Hero 布局"

...(truncated)

files.name: document.md
files.contentPreview:
# Update Documentation Task

**目的**: 在代码更改后更新文档。

## 任务描述

你正在更新代码更改后的文档。

## 1. 识别更改

- 检查 git diff 或最近的提交以了解修改的文件
- 识别更改/添加了哪些功能/模块
- 注意任何新文件、删除的文件或重命名的文件

## 2. 验证当前实现

**关键**: 不要信任现有文档。

对于每个更改的文件：
- 阅读当前实现
- 理解实际行为（不是文档的行为）
- 注意与现有文档的任何差异

## 3. 更新相关文档

### CHANGELOG.md
在"Unreleased"部分添加条目：
- 使用类别：Added, Changed, Fixed, Security, Deprecated, Removed
- 使用简洁、面向用户的语言
- 包含相关的 issue/PR 引用

[code block]

### README.md
如果适用，更新：
- 功能列表
- 使用示例
- API 参考
- 配置选项
- 安装/设置步骤

### API 文档
如果有 API 更改：
- 更新端点描述
- 更新请求/响应示例
- 记录新参数或更改的参数
- 更新错误代码和消息

### 代码注释
确保：
- 函数/类有 JSDoc/文档字符串
- 复杂逻辑有内联注释
- 公共 API 有清晰的使用示例

## 4. 文档风格规则

- **简洁**: 直达重点，避免冗长
- **面向用户**: 关注用户需要知道什么，而不是实现细节
- **示例**: 在适当的地方包含代码示例
- **更新**: 删除过时的信息
- **一致**: 遵循现有的文档风格和格式

## 5. 文档检查清单

完成前验证：
- [ ] CHANGELOG 有所有用户可见的更改
- [ ] README 反映当前功能
- [ ] API 文档是最新的
- [ ] 代码注释是清晰和准确的
- [ ] 示例代码可以工作
- [ ] 没有断开的链接或引用
- [ ] 版本号是正确的（如适用）

## 输出格式

[code block]markdown
[显示将添加到 CHANGELOG 的确切条目]
[code block]

## 重要原则

1. **准确性优于完整性**: 宁可跳过文档也不要记录错误的内容
2. **面向用户**: 从用户的角度写作，而不是实现者
3. **保持同步**: 文档应该反映代码，而不是代码应该做什么
4. **版本化**: 如果是破坏性更改，要明确
5. **可搜索**: 使用清晰的术语和关键词

## 特殊情况

### 破坏性更改
- 在 CHANGELOG 中明确标记
- 提供迁移指南
- 包含"之前"和"之后"的示例

### 新功能
- 添加功能概述
- 包含使用示例
- 记录任何配置选项

### Bug 修复
- 描述修复的问题
- 如果相关，解释根本原因
- 提及任何行为变化

files.name: execute.md
files.contentPreview:
# Execute — Exe Agent

**目的**: 按计划逐步实现功能，始终在 Blueprint 文档的约束下编码，而不只是盯着 Plan 执行。

## 前置条件
- `./plans/[feature-name].md` — 已经过资深工程师 Review 的开发计划

---

## 第 0 步：Mission Briefing（必须首先执行，不可跳过）

在写任何代码之前，读取以下文档建立完整上下文：

[code block]

**任何与 Tech Spec 或 UI Spec 的偏差，必须先暂停讨论，不得自行决定。**

---

## 实施要求

### 代码质量标准
- **优雅**：代码干净、可读、易于理解
- **最小化**：只构建计划里有的内容，不过度工程化
- **模块化**：严格遵守 Tech Spec 定义的 src 目录结构
- **可测试**：编写可以直接对照 Test Spec 验证的代码
- **文档化**：注释解释"为什么"，尤其是复杂逻辑

### 对齐检查点（每个步骤完成前）
在标记步骤为 ✅ 完成前，确认：
- [ ] 数据字段与 Tech Spec 数据模型一致（无自行添加的字段）
- [ ] UI 实现与 mockup 视觉一致（布局、颜色、间距）
- [ ] API 调用与 Tech Spec 接口设计一致（端点、参数、错误处理）
- [ ] 对应 Test Spec 的 P0 用例可以通过

---

## 实施流程

每个步骤：

**开始前**
- 将步骤状态更新为 🟡 进行中
- 重读 Mission Briefing 中与本步骤相关的部分
- 确认要修改/创建的文件列表

**实施期间**
- 遵循 Tech Spec 定义的现有模式
- 在进行时勾选子任务
- 发现与文档不一致之处：**立即暂停并指出，不要自行假设**

**完成后**
- 执行对齐检查点的 4 项确认
- 将步骤状态更新为 ✅ 完成
- 更新总体进度百分比
- 简述完成内容和任何需要后续关注的点

---

## 进度更新格式

[code block]

---

## 遇到问题时

如果发现：
- 计划中的实现方式与 Tech Spec 有冲突
- UI 需要某个 Tech Spec 没有设计的 API
- 某个需求在 PRD/Tech Spec 中描述模糊

**立即暂停，描述冲突点，等待确认后再继续。** 不要绕过文档自行决定，这是漂移的根源。

files.name: explore.md
files.contentPreview:
# Initial Exploration Stage

**目的**: 在编写任何代码之前理解问题。这是"先思考再构建"的检查点。

## 你的任务

你的任务**不是**立即实现功能，而是充分理解它。

## 你的职责

- 分析并理解现有代码库
- 准确确定这个功能如何集成，包括所有依赖项
- 清楚地识别任何不清楚或模糊的内容
- 列出你需要回答的所有问题或歧义

记住，你的工作**不是**实现（还不是）。只是理解。

请确认你完全理解，我会开始下一阶段。

## 探索检查清单

在继续之前，请回答：

1. **问题理解**
   - 我们要解决什么问题？
   - 为什么需要这个功能？
   - 它如何适应更大的系统？

2. **技术范围**
   - 涉及哪些文件/模块？
   - 有哪些依赖关系？
   - 可能的副作用或影响？

3. **不明确之处**
   - 有什么不清楚的地方？
   - 需要澄清哪些假设？
   - 有哪些边缘情况需要考虑？

4. **现有模式**
   - 代码库中是否已有类似实现？
   - 应该遵循哪些现有模式？
   - 需要考虑哪些架构约束？

## 输出

提供一个简洁的探索摘要：

[code block]

files.name: issue.md
files.contentPreview:
# Create Issue

**目的**: 在开发过程中快速捕获 bug/功能需求

## 使用场景
用户在开发过程中想到了一个 bug/功能需求

## 你的目标

创建一个完整的 issue，包含：
- 清晰的标题
- TL;DR - 简要说明这个 issue 的内容
- 当前状态 vs 预期结果
- 需要修改的相关文件
- 风险/注意事项（如适用）
- 适当的类型/优先级/工作量标签

## 如何实现

**提问**以填补信息空白 - 要简洁、尊重用户的上下文：
- 这个 issue/功能是什么
- 当前行为 vs 期望行为
- 类型（bug/feature/improvement）和优先级（如果还不明确）

保持问题简短。一条消息包含 2-3 个有针对性的问题。

**搜索上下文**仅在有帮助时：
- 查看相关文件以确认当前行为
- 确认它是否已存在于积压工作中
- 注意影响范围

**输出格式**

使用这个模板：

[code block]

创建后：
- 将其保存到项目的 `issues/` 目录
- 确认用户是否希望立即处理它

files.name: learn.md
files.contentPreview:
# Learning Opportunity

**目的**: 当你遇到不理解的东西时，将 AI 切换到教学模式。

## 当前模式

暂停开发模式。用户是一个技术 PM，对这个概念不熟悉。

## 教学方法

**目标受众**: 具有中级经验的技术 PM

**理念**: 80/20 规则 - 专注于提供 80% 理解的概念，而不是每个细节

## 三层次解释

以**三个递增的复杂度**呈现概念：

### Level 1: 核心概念
- 这是什么，为什么它存在
- 它解决什么问题
- 你什么时候会使用这个模式
- 它如何适应更广泛的架构

### Level 2: 工作原理
- 底层机制
- 关键的权衡和为什么我们选择这个方法
- 与替代方案的比较
- 实际使用时需要知道的内容

### Level 3: 深入探讨（可选）
- 技术实现细节
- 边缘情况和细微差别
- 高级模式和优化
- 生产环境中的常见陷阱

## 教学结构

[code block][语言]
// 展示它实际应用的清晰示例
[code block]

## 教学原则

1. **从"为什么"开始**: 在"如何"之前先解释原因
2. **使用类比**: 将复杂概念与熟悉的概念联系起来
3. **展示，不只是说**: 包含代码示例和视觉效果
4. **承认复杂性**: 对真正复杂的事物保持诚实
5. **提供路径**: 给出清晰的下一步学习步骤

## 互动

解释后，问：

[code block]

## 注意事项

- **不要居高临下**: 尊重用户的智力
- **不要假设**: 定义技术术语
- **不要过载**: 一次保持一个概念
- **要鼓励**: 让学习感觉可以实现

记住：目标是**理解**，而不是记忆每个细节。

files.name: peer-review.md
files.contentPreview:
# Peer Review Task

**目的**: 让不同的 AI 模型审查彼此的工作。我会让 Codex 或 Gemini 审查 Claude 编写的代码，然后将反馈带回。开发负责人验证每个发现，而不是盲目接受。

## 审查者角色

公司内的另一个团队负责人审查了这段代码。

关键点：
- **他们对这个项目的上下文了解较少**
- **你是团队负责人** - 不要盲目接受发现
- **你的工作是批判性地评估每个发现**

## 来自同行审查的反馈

[从其他模型粘贴反馈]

---

## 你的职责

对于上面的**每个**发现：

1. **验证它是否存在** - 实际检查代码
2. **如果不存在** - 清楚地解释为什么（可能引用现有代码）
3. **如果存在** - 评估严重程度并添加你自己的上下文

### 评估标准

对于每个发现：
- ✅ **有效**: 这是一个真正的问题，应该修复
- ⚠️ **部分有效**: 有些道理，但需要上下文
- ❌ **无效**: 这不是问题/已经处理/基于错误理解
- 💭 **需要讨论**: 需要架构决策或澄清

## 分析后提供

[code block]

## 重要提示

1. **不要防御性**: 如果批评有效，承认它
2. **不要盲目接受**: 验证每个声明
3. **提供证据**: 用代码引用支持你的分析
4. **保持客观**: 关注技术价值，不关注谁是对的
5. **增加价值**: 为每个发现提供上下文和细微差别

记住：目标是产生最好的代码，而不是"赢得"审查。

files.name: review-mockup.md
files.contentPreview:
# Review Mockup Against UI Specifications

**目的**: 自动验证 `./.blueprint/mockup/` 中生成的 Mockup 代码是否符合 `./.blueprint/UI_Spec.md` 的设计规范，并生成详细的 Review 报告。

## 前置条件
- 必须存在：`./.blueprint/UI_Spec.md`（UI 设计规范）
- 必须存在：`./.blueprint/mockup/`（Mockup 代码）

## 产出
- Review 报告（每次新文件，不覆盖旧报告）：
  - `./reports/mockup-reviews/MOCKUP_REVIEW_REPORT_v{n}_{YYYYMMDD_HHmmss}.md`
  - 示例：`./reports/mockup-reviews/MOCKUP_REVIEW_REPORT_v3_20260224_143000.md`
- 最新报告索引（可覆盖更新）：`./reports/mockup-reviews/LATEST.md`
- 包含：符合项、不符合项、建议改进项、可选截图对比、版本标识

---

## Review Agent System Prompt

[code block]text
❌ 错误: 缺少必要文件

缺失:
- ./.blueprint/UI_Spec.md
  或
- ./.blueprint/mockup/

请先生成这些文件后再运行 Review
[code block]markdown
#### [优先级] [问题标题]
**位置**:
- `path/to/file.ext`（可附行号）

**UI Spec 要求**:
[code block]

**当前实现**:
[code block]

**差异说明**:
- [具体差异点 1]
- [具体差异点 2]

**修复建议**:
[code block]
[code block]text
✅ Review 完成！

📄 报告保存路径: ./reports/mockup-reviews/MOCKUP_REVIEW_REPORT_v{n}_{YYYYMMDD_HHmmss}.md
🆔 报告版本: v{n}
🕒 生成时间: {YYYY-MM-DD HH:mm:ss}
📊 总体评分: X/10

🔴 高优先级问题: N 个
🟡 中优先级问题: N 个
🟢 低优先级问题: N 个

💡 建议:
1. 先修复高优先级问题
2. 修复后重新运行 Review Agent
3. 人工设计验收确认
[code block]`

---

## 使用说明（给命令调用者）

在项目根目录执行本命令后，Agent 应：
1. 读取 `./.blueprint/UI_Spec.md`
2. 扫描 `./.blueprint/mockup/` 代码
3. 生成版本化报告到 `./reports/mockup-reviews/`（不覆盖历史）
4. 更新 `./reports/mockup-reviews/LATEST.md`
5. 在控制台输出简版总结（评分 + 问题计数 + 版本号 + 下一步）

files.name: review.md
files.contentPreview:
# Review — Code Review + Drift Detection

**目的**: 开发完成后进行双维度审查：代码质量审查 + 实现与 Blueprint 规格的漂移检测。两者缺一不可。

## 前置条件
- `.blueprint/Tech_Spec.md`
- `.blueprint/UI_Spec.md`
- `.blueprint/PRD.md`
- `.blueprint/Test_Spec.md`
- `.blueprint/mockup/`

---

## 第 0 步：读取 Blueprint 规格（必须首先执行）

[code block]

---

## 维度一：漂移检测（Drift Detection）

### 1A — Tech Spec 对照表

对照 Tech Spec 的数据模型和 API 设计，检查实现：

| 检查项 | Tech Spec 定义 | 实际实现 | 状态 |
|-------|--------------|---------|------|
| 数据模型字段 | User { id, name, email } | ... | ✅ Aligned / 🔴 Drifted |
| API 端点 | POST /auth/login | ... | ✅ / 🔴 |
| src 目录结构 | src/services/auth.ts | ... | ✅ / ⚠️ |
| 错误处理格式 | { code, message } | ... | ✅ / 🔴 |

### 1B — UI Spec + Mockup 对照表

对照 UI Spec 和 Mockup，检查前端实现：

| 页面/组件 | UI Spec/Mockup 规格 | 实际实现 | 状态 |
|---------|-------------------|---------|------|
| 登录页布局 | 居中卡片，最大宽度 400px | ... | ✅ / 🔴 |
| Button 主色 | #1A56DB | ... | ✅ / ⚠️ |
| 错误状态提示 | 红色 border + 错误文字 | ... | ✅ / 🔴 |

### 1C — PRD 验收标准对照

| PRD 验收标准 | 实现状态 | 证据 |
|------------|---------|------|
| Given 用户登录/When 密码错误/Then 显示错误提示 | ✅ / ❌ | [文件:行] |

**漂移项结论**：
- 🔴 严重漂移（与规格直接冲突）：[N 个]
- ⚠️ 轻微漂移（实现可用但偏离规格）：[N 个]
- ✅ 完全对齐：[N 个]

---

## 维度二：代码质量审查

### 日志记录
- 没有 `console.log` 语句，使用适当的日志记录器
- 日志级别适当（info / warn / error / debug）

### 错误处理
- 异步操作使用 try-catch
- 错误处理得当且信息丰富，格式符合 Tech Spec 定义
- 中心化错误处理

### TypeScript
- 没有 `any` 类型，使用 Tech Spec 中定义的接口/类型
- 类型定义清晰且准确
- 泛型使用得当

### 生产就绪性
- 没有调试语句，没有注释掉的代码
- 没有硬编码的值，使用配置

### React / Hooks
- 副作用有清理，依赖项正确
- 没有无限重渲染的风险...(truncated)

files.name: test.md
files.contentPreview:
# Test Stage

**目的**：严格参照 `.blueprint/Test_Spec.md` 执行测试计划（P0→P1→P2），完成自动化+人工回归，并产出**可发布的测试报告**与**缺陷清单**（含证据与结论）。

## 执行要求

现在请你作为项目 QA / 测试执行负责人，**按 `.blueprint/Test_Spec.md` 完整执行**，并遵循以下规则：

### 基本规则

- **以 `.blueprint/Test_Spec.md` 为唯一测试来源**：不擅自改用例口径；如发现用例与实现不一致，记录为缺陷或阻塞项。
- **不允许“口头通过”**：每条用例必须有**可核验证据**（命令输出、日志片段、截图/录屏、网络请求信息等）。
- **优先级与门禁**：先保证 **P0 100% 执行并判定**；P1/P2 依资源执行；按 Test Spec 的 Release Gate 给出最终结论。
- **环境分层**：能自动化的先自动化；同一类核心链路至少在**本地**与**Vercel Preview（如可用）**各跑一轮（无法访问则标记为阻塞与待补测）。
- **失败处理**：
  - 明确是测试/脚手架/数据问题：可做最小修复并复跑。
  - 明确是产品/实现缺陷：记录为缺陷（含复现步骤与证据），不要“改需求让它通过”。

### 执行流程

每个阶段都要输出进度与产物（不要等到最后才汇总）：

1. **准备阶段**
   - 识别实际项目目录（例如 monorepo 子目录），确认 Node/包管理器版本与依赖安装方式。
   - 读取 `.blueprint/Test_Spec.md`，提取：
     - P0/P1/P2 用例清单（Functional/UI/API/NFT）
     - 关键环境变量与第三方依赖（Slack/Resend 等）
     - 通过标准（Release Gate / DoD）
   - 创建本次运行的测试记录文档：`reports/test-runs/Test_Run_YYYY-MM-DD.md`（或仓库已有约定则沿用）。

2. **基线检查（自动化优先）**
   - 执行并记录：`lint`、`typecheck`、`unit`、`integration`（若项目脚本存在则使用脚本）。
   - 对 `/api/leads` 相关路径重点验证 201/400/429/500 的响应契约与不泄露敏感信息。

3. **E2E / 关键链路回归（P0 必做最小集）**
   - 覆盖 `Test_Spec.md` 的 E2E 最小集（Tours→Detail→Book→ThankYou + 关键失败路径 + 429 限流提示）。
   - 如已有 Playwright：直接跑；如未具备：以最小改动补齐并只实现 P0 smoke（避免过度工程化）。

4. **人工回归（按 Spec 清单执行）**
   - 按 `Test_Spec.md` 的 FT/UI/NFT 清单逐条执行与判定。
   - 对“口径一致性/可复制政策块/China-ready 披露/反爬展示”等高风险点做重点证据留存（复制文本对比、截图、页面元素检查等）。

5. **汇总与门禁结论**
   - 给出 Release Gate 结论（PASS/FAIL/BLOCKED），并明确阻塞项与风险项。
   - 输出可执行的回归建议（需要复测的用例 ID 列表）。

## 进度更新格式（每个阶段都要更...(truncated)

# Verify — Global Context Verifier

**目的**: 在所有定义文档生成完毕后、开发计划制定前，跨文档检查一致性，发现矛盾和遗漏，输出完整性评分。这是最低成本发现问题的时机。

## 触发时机

在以下所有文档都生成并经人工确认后运行：
- `.blueprint/PRD.md` ✅
- `.blueprint/UI_Spec.md` ✅
- `.blueprint/mockup/` ✅（经设计师确认）
- `.blueprint/Tech_Spec.md` ✅（经资深工程师 Review）
- `.blueprint/Test_Spec.md` ✅（经测试工程师 Review）

aiResult.assessment: This is a well-structured engineering workflow system with clear agent roles and pipeline stages, but its heavy reliance on Chinese-language content significantly reduces its utility for English-prompted AI assistants. The documentation excels at defining sequential dependencies and validation checkpoints, though it lacks concrete code patterns and test validation rules that would prevent hallucinations during implementation.
hallucinationRisks: Mixed Chinese/English content may cause AI to misinterpret or ignore critical instructions when prompted in English
No explicit 'must/always/never' rules for code generation patterns, increasing risk of inconsistent implementations
Lack of concrete validation schemas or test assertion examples for verifying AI-generated output
No edge case handling patterns or error boundary specifications for common failure modes
strengths: Clear sequential pipeline with explicit preconditions for each stage reduces context confusion
Well-defined agent roles and responsibilities create predictable workflow boundaries
Version management rules for PRD files prevent AI from overwriting or misinterpreting document history
Drift detection checklists provide concrete alignment verification points between specs and implementation
improvements: Translate all documentation to English to ensure consistent AI comprehension across prompts
Add concrete code generation rules (e.g., 'always use TypeScript strict mode', 'never use any type')
Include validation patterns with specific test assertions and expected outputs
Add error handling templates and edge case documentation for common implementation scenarios
recommendations: Translate all 17 files to English, maintaining the same structure but with English-only content
Add a 'Code Generation Rules' section with 10-15 concrete 'must/always/never' directives for AI coding
Include 3-5 complete validation examples showing expected vs actual output for key checkpoints
Add a 'Common Pitfalls' section documenting frequent AI hallucination patterns and how to prevent them

files.name: estimation-skill.md
files.contentPreview:
---
name: adtech-estimation-skill
description: Structured process for turning an adtech ask into a defensible engineering estimate, with archetype-based sizing, risk multipliers, and reference calibration.
---


## 1. Purpose

This skill turns an adtech ask ("we want a creative builder", "we need to integrate AppLovin MAX", "build us an SSP") into a defensible engineering estimate that 57Blocks is willing to commit to in a fixed-bid SOW or a milestone-based T&M plan.

It exists because adtech estimates fail in characteristic ways:

- **Hidden integrations.** A "simple" creative is actually 4 ad-network specs, 2 MMP postbacks, and a privacy-consent layer.
- **Latency cliffs.** A 100ms RTB SLA forces architectural decisions that double effort vs a 500ms SLA.
- **Spec drift.** SKAN, Privacy Sandbox, ATT, GPP — the spec the client read last year is not the current spec. Estimating without checking version costs 1–3 weeks per surprise.
- **Reconciliation tax.** Every adtech project ends up needing to reconcile internal numbers vs platform dashboards vs MMP — and this is never in the original ask.

This skill gives an engineering-led estimator a structured way to surface those costs *before* signing.

---

## 2. When to use

| Trigger | What you produce |
|---|---|
| Pre-sales discovery call done | Rough order-of-magnitude (ROM) range — "small / medium / large" with ±50% bounds |
| Blueprint PRD generation | Per-epic estimate with risk multipliers for SOW |
| Mid-build re-estimatio...(truncated)

## Critical Reading Path (AI Decision Workflow)

1. **NEW INTEGRATION?** → Read §1 (version state), §2 (transport), Quick start
2. **IMPLEMENTING MEDIA BUY?** → Read §4.1, §5 (Trust), §6.4 (state machine), §8.2 (error codes)
3. **GOVERNANCE / REGULATED VERTICALS?** → Read §8 (especially §8.1: EU AI Act)
4. **TROUBLESHOOTING?** → Jump to §8.2 (error codes), then §6 (key concepts)
5. **VERSION MIGRATION?** → Read §1 (deprecation timeline), §13 (watch-list)

---

AdCP is an open standard that lets **AI agents** discover ad inventory, buy media, build/distribute creative, activate audiences, set up accounts, and run governance/brand-suitability — across CTV, display, audio, social,...(truncated)

files.name: app-tracking-transparency.md
files.contentPreview:
# App Tracking Transparency (ATT)

> **Type:** iOS Privacy Policy and Identity Framework
> **Custodian:** Apple
> **Current Version:** iOS 14.5+ ATT
> **Status:** Required for accessing IDFA in iOS apps

---

## Purpose

App Tracking Transparency (ATT) regulates access to the Identifier for Advertisers (IDFA) on iOS devices. It requires apps to request explicit user permission before collecting or sharing data that tracks users across apps and websites owned by other companies.

>> KEY: ATT is not optional for IDFA access. If the app wants IDFA, it must request permission; otherwise, it must use privacy-safe alternatives.

---

## Core Concepts

| Concept | Definition | Why it matters |
|---|---|---|
| **IDFA** | Identifier for Advertisers on iOS | Primary cross-app ad identifier if permitted |
| **ATT prompt** | System dialog asking user to allow tracking | Mandatory before IDFA access |
| **Authorized** | User allowed tracking | IDFA available to the app |
| **Denied** | User rejected tracking | App must use IDFA zeros; no cross-app tracking |
| **Not determined** | User has not yet been prompted | App may choose when to request |
| **Restricted** | Parental controls or device restrictions | Tracking is disallowed by system |
| **Transparency** | App privacy details in App Store | Required for UI and user trust |
| **Tracking** | Data collection across apps/sites | Defined broadly by Apple |

---

## ATT Request Flow

1. App launches or reaches tracking point
2. App display...(truncated)

files.name: mobile-ads-mediation.md
files.contentPreview:
# Mobile Ads Mediation

> **Type:** Mobile Monetization Architecture
> **Custodian:** Mediation Platforms (AdMob, ironSource, MoPub/Advanced Bidding)
> **Status:** Core for app monetization and yield optimization
> **Scope:** SDK mediation, waterfall, in-app bidding, network optimization

---

## Purpose

Mobile ads mediation manages multiple ad networks inside a single app. It optimizes revenue by selecting the highest-paying network for each impression, ensuring better fill rates and simplified SDK integration.

>> KEY: Mediation is not the ad network itself. It is the orchestration layer that decides which network serves each request.

---

## Core Concepts

| Concept | Definition | Why it matters |
|---|---|---|
| **Mediation SDK** | Platform embedded in an app to manage networks | Reduces integration overhead |
| **Waterfall** | Ordered list of ad networks by priority | Older but still common technique |
| **In-app bidding** | Real-time auction among networks for each impression | Higher yield than waterfall |
| **Ad source** | One network or demand partner in mediation | Can be SDK-based or bid-based |
| **eCPM floor** | Minimum effective CPM required for an ad source | Protects yield from low bids |
| **Fill rate** | Percentage of ad requests that result in ads | Key publisher metric |
| **Latency** | Time to return an ad bid or creative | High latency hurts user experience |
| **Auction** | Real-time competition for an impression | Maximizes revenue if implemented cor...(truncated)

files.name: mobile-attribution-mmp.md
files.contentPreview:
# Mobile Attribution & MMPs

> **Type:** Mobile Measurement and Attribution
> **Custodian:** Mobile Measurement Partners (AppsFlyer, Adjust, Branch, Singular)
> **Status:** Core mobile adtech infrastructure
> **Scope:** App install attribution, deep linking, re-engagement, fraud signals

---

## Purpose

Mobile measurement partners (MMPs) provide centralized attribution for app install campaigns and post-install events. They help advertisers answer:
- Which ad network drove this install?
- Did the user convert after clicking the ad?
- Is this traffic legitimate or fraudulent?
- Was the deep link delivered correctly?

>> KEY: MMPs are not identity stores. They connect click/install data using device signals and privacy-safe identifiers.

---

## Core Concepts

| Concept | Definition | Why it matters |
|---|---|---|
| **Install attribution** | Assigning an app install to a campaign or source | Primary mobile measurement outcome |
| **Click-through attribution** | User clicked an ad before install | Most common attribution mode |
| **View-through attribution** | User saw ad, later installed | Used for display/video campaigns |
| **Deep link** | URL that opens app to a specific screen | Improves engagement and conversion |
| **Deferred deep link** | Deep link that applies after install | Critical for new user onboarding |
| **Retention event** | In-app action after install | Measures quality beyond install |
| **SKAdNetwork** | Apple privacy attribution framework | Privacy-safe i...(truncated)

files.name: mobile-fraud-ivt.md
files.contentPreview:
# Mobile Fraud & Invalid Traffic (IVT)

> **Type:** Mobile Ad Quality and Fraud Prevention
> **Custodian:** Ad Verification / Fraud Detection Platforms
> **Status:** Critical risk area for mobile app advertising
> **Scope:** Click fraud, install fraud, SDK spoofing, bot traffic

---

## Purpose

Mobile invalid traffic (IVT) covers any ad interactions or installs that are not genuine user engagement. Preventing IVT protects advertisers from wasted spend and publishers from revenue loss due to fraud deductions.

>> KEY: IVT is not just "bad users." It includes automated bots, SDK manipulation, click injection, and fake installs.

---

## Core Concepts

| Concept | Definition | Why it matters |
|---|---|---|
| **Invalid Traffic (IVT)** | Impressions or clicks not from real users | Degrades campaign ROI |
| **Click Injection** | Fraudster triggers click event right before install | Falsely credits the fraudster network |
| **Click Flooding** | Many fake clicks to hijack attribution | Overwhelms legitimate clicks |
| **SDK Spoofing** | Fake SDK reporting installs or events | Creates phantom conversions |
| **Device Farm** | Cloud/emulator devices used to simulate installs | Inflates install volume with no quality |
| **Pixel Stuffing** | Invisible pixel generates false impressions | Counts invalid ad inventory |
| **Traffic Shading** | Sending low-value traffic through high-performing tags | Inflates low-quality supply |
| **Viewability Fraud** | Ads rendered offscreen or hidden |...(truncated)

files.contentPreview:
# Mobile Privacy Framework Composition — How Everything Stacks

> **Type:** Mobile Adtech Architecture
> **Status:** Foundational reference for preventing hallucination
> **Audience:** AI agents, privacy engineers, product managers

---

## Problem: Layered Privacy Requirements

Mobile apps don't have ONE privacy framework; they have multiple:
- **OS level** (ATT, GAID access control)
- **App level** (first-party analytics, internal consent)
- **Ad network level** (measurement: SKAdNetwork, Privacy Sandbox)
- **Regulatory level** (GDPR, CCPA, ePrivacy)
- **Data partner level** (third-party audience, MMP consent)

>> HALLUCINATION RISK: AI agents may assume "use Privacy Sandbox" or "use SKAdNetwork" exclusively. WRONG. Stack them all together.

files.name: mraid.md
files.contentPreview:
# MRAID 3.0 — Mobile Rich Media Ad Interface Definitions

> **Type:** Mobile Adtech Reference
> **Current Version:** 3.0 (released 2021, actively maintained)
> **Custodian:** IAB Tech Lab
> **Spec Home:** https://www.iab.com/wp-content/uploads/2021/02/MRAID_3.0_FINAL.pdf
> **Status:** Stable; MRAID 4.0 in discussion

---

## Purpose

Standardizes **bidirectional communication** between rich media creative (banner, interstitial, rewarded, native) and the mobile app or browser container hosting it.

>> CRITICAL: MRAID is NOT web tech. It is mobile-specific; no cross-platform code reuse.

---

## Core Concepts

| Concept | Definition | Implication |
|---|---|---|
| **Container** | The app or mobile web wrapper hosting the ad | Container declares its motion/size/closeable capabilities |
| **Event model** | Two-way messaging between creative JS and container (no cookies for in-app) | Creatives in-app MUST NOT rely on third-party cookies |
| **Viewability** | MRAID tracks ad view state via `onViewableChange` event | Distinct from web OMID; sellers declare which system they support |
| **Device storage** | MRAID supports `localStorage` (scoped to app, not synced) | Retargeting via MRAID storage is app-scoped only; no cross-app access |
| **Native actions** | Creative can invoke OS-level operations (open URL, call phone, etc.) | Container enforces sandbox; not all actions available in all containers |

---

## MRAID 3.0 Required Events & Methods

### Events (Container → Creative)

| ...(truncated)

files.name: omsdk.md
files.contentPreview:
# OMSDK — Open Measurement SDK

> **Type:** Ad Verification & Viewability Framework
> **Custodian:** IAB Tech Lab
> **Current Version:** 1.4.13 (stable); 2.0 in development
> **Transport:** JavaScript (web), Native SDKs (iOS/Android)
> **Use Case:** Third-party ad verification, viewability measurement, fraud detection

---

## Purpose

OMSDK (Open Measurement SDK) is a **standardized interface** that lets independent third-party vendors (double-verify, Moat, Integral Ad Science, etc.) measure ad quality without direct access to publisher or advertiser data.

>> KEY: OMSDK = transparency layer. Creatives + publishers expose events; verification partners listen. No data sharing; events only.

**Why it matters:**
- Buyer verifies ad actually served (not hidden, not spoofed)
- Publisher proves viewability compliance
- Advertiser checks for brand safety
- All without exposing first-party data

---

## Core Concepts

| Concept | Definition | For AI |
|---|---|---|
| **Session** | A single ad impression lifecycle (start → finish) | Created when ad loads; destroyed when closed/navigated |
| **Event** | Standardized signal fired by creative or publisher (e.g., "displayed", "interaction", "error") | Verification partners receive and record them |
| **Listener** | Third-party verification SDK that observes events | IAS, DoubleVerify, Moat SDK instances |
| **Verification** | Partner analyzes events + renders proof of viewability | e.g., "Ad was 50% visible for 1+ second = viewable" |
| ...(truncated)

files.name: playable-ads.md
files.contentPreview:
# Playable Ads — Interactive In-App Ad Experience

> **Type:** Mobile Adtech Creative Format
> **Common Platforms:** iOS, Android, mobile web
> **Status:** Industry standard for user acquisition (app installs)
> **Use Case:** Game/app demos, interactive product try-outs before install

---

## Purpose

Playable ads are **interactive mini-games or app simulations** embedded directly in ad placements (banners, interstitials, fullscreen). Users interact with the playable content within the ad—without leaving the app or installing yet—then tap "Install" if interested.

>> KEY: Playable = engagement before install decision. Higher conversion rates than static creative.

---

## Core Concepts

| Concept | Definition | For Ad Networks |
|---|---|---|
| **Playable** | Interactive HTML5/WebGL game or demo running in a sandbox | Loaded into MRAID container; must NOT exceed timeout |
| **Engagement tracking** | Track user interactions (taps, swipes, game score) to predict conversion | Critical for attribution; drives quality scoring |
| **End card** | Post-gameplay screen with "Install" CTA; appears after timeout or user completion | Seller controls; buyer cannot modify |
| **Timeout** | Max playable duration before auto-advancing to end card | Typical: 20–60 seconds; depends on placement |
| **Containment** | Playable runs in iframe/sandbox; cannot access user data, device ID, or app storage | Privacy + security by design |
| **Store redirect** | Tap "Install" → routes to App Store (iO...(truncated)

files.name: skadnetwork.md
files.contentPreview:
# SKAdNetwork — Apple Privacy-Preserving App Install Attribution

> **Type:** Mobile Attribution (iOS)
> **Current Version:** SKAdNetwork 4.0+ (required iOS 14.5+)
> **Custodian:** Apple

files.contentPreview:
# StoreKit 2 — Apple In-App Purchase Framework

> **Type:** Mobile Monetization (iOS)
> **Current Version:** StoreKit 2 (iOS 15.1+, required as of 2024)
> **Custodian:** Apple
> **Spec Home:** https://developer.apple.com/storekit/
> **Legacy:** StoreKit 1 deprecated; new apps MUST use StoreKit 2

---

## Purpose

Handles **in-app transactions** (one-time purchases, subscriptions, in-app currency) on iOS with secure receipt validation and subscription lifecycle management.

>> CRITICAL FOR ADTECH: Rewarded video ads frequently grant in-app currency or unlock subscriptions. Creatives MUST NOT bypass StoreKit; direct payment links violate App Store policy.

---

## Core Concepts

| Concept | Definition | For Adtech |
|---|---|---|
| **Product** | An in-app item or subscription offer | Defined in App Store Connect; identified by `productID` |
| **Transaction** | A purchase or subscription action | Initiated via `AppStore.sync()` or `Product.purchase()` |
| **Receipt** | Cryptographic proof of purchase | Validated server-side; contains purchase date, expiry, refund info |
| **Subscription** | Recurring charge (auto-renew) | Includes renewal state, grace period, refund window |
| **Entitlement** | Unlocked feature after purchase | Verified by app; adtech creative must check entitlement before granting reward |

---

files.name: proposal-skill.md
files.contentPreview:
---
name: adtech-proposal-skill
description: Structured guidance for turning an adtech ask into a defensible 57Blocks proposal, including discovery, archetype selection, architecture, and narrative.
---

## 1. Purpose

This skill turns a vague adtech ask into a defensible 57Blocks proposal — fast, opinionated, and grounded in patterns we have actually shipped. It is paired with the **Adtech Estimation Skill** (which handles numbers); this skill handles **scoping, architecture, and narrative**.

It exists because adtech clients almost always describe what they *think* they want ("a creative builder", "an ad network", "an MMP integration") rather than the system they actually need. A 57Blocks proposal must close that gap *in the proposal itself* — not after signing — because the gap is where margin and trust both die.

The skill produces three artifacts:

1. A **discovery transcript** — the answers to the structured question battery.
2. A **scoped solution** — archetype, reference architecture, included/excluded scope, risks.
3. A **commercial wrapper** — team, timeline, milestones, assumptions.

---

## 2. When to use

| Trigger | What you produce |
|---|---|
| First inbound from a prospect | Discovery question battery (§4); 30-min call agenda |
| After discovery call | Solution memo (§5–§9); 1-page architecture diagram |
| RFP response due | Full proposal using §10 output template |
| Client expansion / new SOW | Skip discovery, go straight to §5 archetype check |

---

## 3....(truncated)

aiResult.assessment: This adtech knowledge area provides deep, accurate domain coverage across mobile advertising, privacy frameworks, and attribution systems, which is critical for preventing AI hallucinations in adtech projects. However, it lacks practical code examples, integration patterns, and quick-start templates that would accelerate MVP delivery. The documentation is strong on conceptual accuracy but weak on actionable implementation guidance.
hallucinationRisks: AI may assume SKAdNetwork or Privacy Sandbox is used exclusively, missing the layered privacy stack (ATT, GDPR, MMP, etc.)
AI may generate code that bypasses StoreKit for rewarded video currency grants, violating App Store policy
AI may treat MRAID as cross-platform web tech instead of mobile-specific, leading to incorrect creative container implementations
strengths: Comprehensive coverage of mobile adtech standards (MRAID, OMSDK, SKAdNetwork, StoreKit) with clear versioning and custodians
Explicit hallucination risk warnings (e.g., 'HALLUCINATION RISK: AI agents may assume...') that directly guide AI behavior
Structured estimation and proposal skills that provide archetype-based sizing and risk multipliers for project scoping
improvements: Add code snippets for common integration patterns (e.g., MMP postback handling, OMSDK event subscription)
Include quick-start templates for MVP features like a basic mediation waterfall or SKAdNetwork postback parser
Provide explicit 'do not' rules (e.g., 'Never hardcode IDFA without ATT check') to reduce hallucination risk
recommendations: Add a 'Common Integration Patterns' section with pseudo-code for MMP attribution, OMSDK verification, and SKAdNetwork postback handling
Create a 'Quick Start: MVP Ad SDK Integration' checklist that lists minimal files and configurations needed
Include explicit 'Anti-Patterns' warnings for each major component (e.g., 'Do not assume SKAdNetwork replaces MMP attribution')

# Fintech & DeFi Vertical Knowledge Library

## Document Metadata
allowed_tag_keys: vertical, regulation, topic
entry_separator: ---
type_allowed_values: constraint, pattern, compliance, convention
dimension_allowed_values: PM, ENGINEERING, BOTH

## Table of Contents
- [Constraints](#constraints)
- [Patterns](#patterns)
- [Compliance](#compliance)
- [Conventions](#conventions)

---

## Constraints

### Decimal-Only Monetary Representation
- type: constraint
- dimension: ENGINEERING
- tags: vertical:fintech | topic:monetary-precision
All monetary values must be stored as arbitrary-precision decimals and serialized as strings to prevent floating-point rounding errors that compound across transactions and lead to reconciliation failures or regulatory audit findings.
ref: Project Jia | IEEE 754 limitation

---

### Idempotent Payment Request Enforcement
- type: constraint
- dimension: ENGINEERING
- tags: vertical:fintech | topic:payment-integration
Every outbound payment must use a client-generated idempotency key persisted before transmission to prevent duplicate disbursements or double-charging during network retries or webhook replays.
ref: Project Jia | Stripe Idempotency Standard

---

### Tiered Investment Allocation Ceilings
- type: constraint
- dimension: PM
- tags: vertical:defi | topic:investment-process,risk-management
Investment review depth must scale with allocation size and fast-track approvals must be capped at 5% of total portfolio value to limit the blast radius...(truncated)

aiResult.assessment: The document provides a solid foundation for fintech domain knowledge with clear constraints and compliance references, but lacks depth in practical implementation patterns and API schemas. The single file approach limits coverage of critical areas like payment processing, KYC/AML workflows, and transaction reconciliation.
hallucinationRisks: Missing concrete API schemas for payment gateways or blockchain interactions may cause AI to invent non-standard interfaces
No examples of decimal serialization patterns could lead to inconsistent implementations across the codebase
Absence of KYC/AML compliance workflows increases risk of generating non-compliant user onboarding flows
strengths: Clear constraint on decimal-only monetary representation with regulatory reference
Structured metadata with allowed tags and dimension values improves AI parsing
Compliance section provides regulatory context that reduces hallucination risk in audit-sensitive areas
improvements: Add concrete code examples for decimal serialization in multiple languages (Python, TypeScript, Rust)
Include payment processing patterns with idempotency keys and retry logic
Provide KYC/AML workflow diagrams with required data fields and verification steps
recommendations: Add a 'Patterns' section with reusable code templates for common fintech operations (balance checks, transfers, fee calculations)
Include API integration examples for major payment processors (Stripe, Plaid) with request/response schemas
Create separate files for sub-domains like payments, lending, and compliance to improve AI retrieval specificity

# Healthcare Requirement Knowledge Base

## 1 Purpose, Audience & How to Use This Doc

This document is the product-management counterpart to the engineering-focused Healthtech Knowledge Base Core. The Core doc tells you *how* to build a healthcare system. This one tells you *what to build, for whom, and what to refuse*.

The lessons here are not neutral best-practice advice. They are opinionated, hard-won conclusions from two multi-year EU healthcare engagements that both worked and hurt. Every rule below was paid for in development time, rework, frustrated nurses, or audit findings. Naming the experience that produced the rule is what makes this doc useful instead of generic.

**Who this is for:**

* **Project leads kicking off a new healthcare engagement** — read end-to-end before scoping. Sections 12.3, 12.5, and 12.7 will save you the most calendar time.  
* **Sales identifying or qualifying new healthcare opportunities** — section 12.8 is the discovery checklist, section 12.9 is the red-flag scan. Run them before the second customer call.  
* **Junior product managers stepping into a healthcare project** — read the whole thing, but pay closest attention to the war stories in sections 12.4 and 12.6. They are the patterns that surprised us the most and that are easiest to repeat if you have not seen them named.

**How to use this doc:**

* Skim by topic if you have a specific question; the lessons are designed to be readable in any order.  
* Bring section 12.8 (Discovery...(truncated)

# Healthtech Mobile App Knowledge base

Source: Based on hands-on experience from two real-world health projects — LevvelHealth App (remote patient monitoring) and Cebreo App (ear-EEG brain monitoring).

Purpose: To document architecture design, hardware integration, data processing, and security compliance knowledge from healthcare software development, showcasing our company’s technical capabilities and project delivery experience in digital health.

---

## 1 Mobile App Case Overview

### 1.1 Levvel Health — Remote Patient Monitoring (RPM) Platform

**Product positioning:** A SaaS remote patient monitoring platform for hospitals and clinics, extending in-hospital monitoring capabilities to the patient’s home, enabling Hospital-at-Home care delivery.

**Target users:**

| User Role | Core Pain Point | Product Value |
| :---- | :---- | :---- |
| **Chronic disease patients** (COPD, heart failure, post-surgical recovery) | Frequent hospital visits required; no real-time access to physician guidance | Self-measure vitals at home; data auto-uploaded to EHR; automatic alerts on anomalies; built-in video/text communication with care teams |
| **Clinicians and nurses** | Manual data entry is time-consuming and error-prone; impossible to monitor large panels of at-home patients simultaneously | AI-driven patient risk prioritization; automatic EWS calculation; automated documentation to reduce paperwork burden |
| **Hospital administrators** | Bed shortages, high readmission rates | ...(truncated)

aiResult.assessment: This healthtech knowledge base provides deep, practical domain expertise from real-world projects, covering compliance, architecture, and mobile app integration. It excels in domain coverage and technical accuracy, but could improve by adding more concrete code examples and structured validation patterns to reduce AI hallucination risks.
hallucinationRisks: Missing explicit code snippets or API schemas may lead AI to invent incorrect implementations
Lack of standardized data models for common health entities (e.g., patient, observation) could cause inconsistent output
No explicit test patterns or edge case handling for compliance-critical paths (e.g., data encryption, audit logs)
strengths: Rich domain coverage with real project examples (RPM, EEG, diabetes) provides context-aware guidance
Clear compliance framework (HIPAA, GDPR) helps AI generate regulation-aware code
Detailed architecture patterns (e.g., offline-first, hardware integration) reduce design errors
improvements: Add concrete code examples for key patterns (e.g., FHIR API integration, encryption utilities)
Include validation checklists for common compliance pitfalls
Provide standardized data model definitions (e.g., JSON schemas for patient records)
recommendations: Add a 'Quick Start' section with a minimal working example (e.g., patient data CRUD with FHIR)
Include a 'Common Mistakes' list with anti-patterns to avoid
Create a reference table of regulatory requirements mapped to code-level actions

aiResult.assessment: This knowledge area is completely empty with no files or sub-directories, providing zero domain knowledge or business logic for AI assistants to reference. Without any content, the AI has no guidance on Web3-specific concepts, protocols, or implementation patterns, making it impossible to generate accurate or useful code.
hallucinationRisks: No domain definitions or terminology to prevent AI from inventing incorrect Web3 concepts
Missing API schemas and data models for blockchain interactions, leading to fabricated interfaces
Absence of compliance or security rules for smart contracts and token standards
No integration patterns for wallets, exchanges, or DeFi protocols
Lack of test requirements or validation patterns for Web3-specific edge cases
improvements: Add comprehensive Web3 domain documentation covering blockchain fundamentals, token standards (ERC-20, ERC-721, ERC-1155), and smart contract development patterns
Include practical code examples for wallet connection, transaction signing, and event listening
Document security best practices for handling private keys, gas estimation, and reentrancy attacks
Provide integration guides for popular Web3 libraries (ethers.js, web3.js, viem) and frameworks (Hardhat, Foundry)
recommendations: Create a structured directory with files covering: blockchain basics, smart contract patterns, token standards, wallet integration, DeFi protocols, and security guidelines
Include concrete API schemas and data models for common Web3 operations (transactions, events, contract calls)
Add test patterns and validation rules specific to blockchain development (e.g., handling transaction failures, gas limits)

# Annotation Scheme Design Reference / 数据标注方案设计参考

> Loaded on demand by `ai-model-lifecycle` SKILL.md when the user is designing or running annotation work.

---

# English

## 2.1 Annotation Guideline Development

The guideline is the foundation of data quality. **Finalize and team-review before annotation begins.**

The guideline document should contain:

- **Task definition**: plain-language description with examples
- **Label taxonomy**:
  - Name, definition, boundary description for every label
  - Hierarchical relationships (if any)
  - Rules for distinguishing easily confused labels (most critical part — drives consistency)
- **Annotation rules**:
  - Specific positive / negative example criteria
  - Rules for ambiguous cases (prefer false negatives vs false positives? what occlusion ratio counts as invisible?)
  - Special cases (truncated objects, tiny objects, dense overlaps)
- **Annotation examples**:
  - At least 10 positive + 5 negative + 5 edge case examples
  - Each example annotated with explanation
- **Quality requirements**:
  - Expected inter-annotator agreement (IAA)
  - Acceptance criteria

## 2.2 Annotation Tool Selection

| Task type | Recommended tools |
|-----------|------------------|
| Image classification | Label Studio, CVAT |
| Object detection / segmentation | CVAT, Labelme, Roboflow |
| OCR / text detection | PPOCRLabel, Label Studio |
| NER / text classification | Doccano, Label Studio, Prodigy |
| Multimodal / custom | Label Studio (most flex...(truncated)

# Data Preparation Reference / 数据准备参考

> Loaded on demand by `ai-model-lifecycle` SKILL.md when the user is at the data-prep stage.

---

# English

## 1.1 Data Requirement Analysis

Before collecting any data, answer:

- **Task type**: Classification / Detection / Segmentation / OCR / NER / Generation / Recommendation / Other
- **Data modality**: Images, text, audio, video, tabular, multimodal
- **Data source**:
  - Business system logs (most realistic; may have privacy concerns)
  - Public datasets (fast start; possible domain gap)
  - Web scraping (consider legal compliance)
  - Synthetic data / augmentation (long-tail supplementation)
  - Manual collection (expensive; quality-controllable)
- **Data volume estimates** (ballpark, depends on task difficulty and model size):
  - Image classification: 500–5,000 per class
  - Object detection: 1,000–10,000 annotated instances per class
  - NER: 5,000–50,000 annotated sentences
  - LLM fine-tune: 1,000–100,000 high-quality instruction pairs

## 1.2 Data Collection Strategy

**Principle: coverage first, volume second.**

- Cover all business scenarios and edge cases first
- Mirror real production distribution as closely as possible
- Record per-class ratio if class imbalance exists (handle later in training)
- Establish data version control (DVC or similar); track every data change

**Data quality checklist:**
- [ ] Consistent formats (image size/encoding, text encoding, file naming)
- [ ] Duplicates removed
- [ ] Corrupted/inval...(truncated)

**Canary release (recommended):**

[code block]

- Observe each stage for 1–3 days; expand only after metrics confirmed stable

**A/B Testing:**
- For scenarios with clear business metrics (recommendation, search), use A/B testing to evaluate impact on business KPIs
- Random traffic allocation between experiment and control groups, sufficient sample size
- Typical experiment duration: 1–2 weeks

## 7.3 Online Monitoring

**Metrics to monitor:**

- **Model metrics**: prediction confidence distribution, per-class prediction ratio, anomalous output rate

# Multi-Round Iteration Reference / 多轮迭代闭环参考

> Loaded on demand by `ai-model-lifecycle` SKILL.md when the user is planning or executing iteration rounds.

---

# English

## 8.1 Iteration Triggers

A new iteration should be triggered when:

- Online monitoring detects metric degradation (data drift, new scenarios)
- Bad cases reported by business stakeholders accumulate beyond a threshold
- New business scenarios require model support
- Better models / methods become available
- Periodic schedule (recommend at least once every 1–3 months)

## 8.2 Standard Iteration Workflow

[code block]

## 8.3 Bad Case Analysis Methodology

Bad case analysis is the engine that drives iteration.

**Steps:**

1. **Collect**: gather error samples from production logs, user feedback, QA testing
2. **Classify**: categorize errors into:
   - Data issue (scenarios / distributions not in training set)
   - Annotation error (training labels were wrong)
   - Model capacity gap (model has seen similar samples but predicted incorrectly)
   - Preprocessing / post-processing bug (transformation caused issue)
   - Extreme edge case (very rare — may not be worth solving)
3. **Quantify**: compute proportion of each category; identify dominant type
4. **Plan**: develop strategy targeting the dominant error category
5. **Verify**: re-evaluate after optimization; confirm improvement on the targeted error type

## 8.4 Iteration Log

Record one log per iteration round. **Do not reconstruct the structure from me...(truncated)

files.name: model-selection.md
files.contentPreview:
# Model Selection Reference / 模型选型参考

> Loaded on demand by `ai-model-lifecycle` SKILL.md when the user is choosing a model.

---

# English

## 3.1 Decision Framework

Selection is not "pick the best model" — it's **"pick the most suitable model under current constraints."**

| Dimension | Considerations |
|-----------|---------------|
| Accuracy | Public benchmark performance, domain adaptability |
| Speed | Inference latency, throughput, business SLA |
| Resources | Training GPU, inference hardware, VRAM footprint |
| Maturity | Production track record, community activity, maintenance |
| Maintainability | Code quality, docs, team tech-stack alignment |
| Data efficiency | Few-shot performance, pretrained-weight availability |
| Extensibility | Ease of adding new classes / scenarios |

## 3.2 Three-Step Selection Strategy

1. **Baseline**: pick a mature, simple, quickly deployable solution to run the full pipeline first. Don't chase SOTA out of the gate.
   - Purpose: validate data quality and pipeline feasibility quickly
   - Examples: ResNet-50 for image classification, YOLOv8-m for detection, BERT-base for NER

2. **Candidates**: after baseline validation, experiment with 2–3 stronger options
   - Compare: accuracy gain vs. speed/resource cost
   - Controlled experiments: same data, same preprocessing, same evaluation protocol

3. **Production**: pick final solution based on accuracy, speed, resources, maintenance cost
   - Apply compression (distillation / pruning / qu...(truncated)

# Model Optimization Reference / 模型优化参考

> Loaded on demand by `ai-model-lifecycle` SKILL.md when the user is improving an existing model.

---

# English

## 5.1 Accuracy Optimization (Improving Performance)

**Optimization levers, ranked by typical priority:**

1. **Data optimization (highest priority — usually the most effective)**
   - Analyze bad cases; identify error patterns (errors concentrated in specific scenes / lighting / classes)
   - Targeted data supplementation: additional collection + re-annotation
   - Clean annotation errors: use model predictions to flag potentially mislabeled samples
   - Increase data diversity: add edge cases and hard cases

2. **Training strategy optimization**
   - Hyperparameter tuning: LR, batch size, optimizer, scheduler
   - Better augmentation combinations
   - Better loss functions (task-specific)
   - Pretrained weight selection (larger / better pretraining dataset)

3. **Model architecture optimization**
   - Stronger backbone
   - Adjust neck / head architecture
   - Add attention mechanisms
   - Model ensemble

4. **Post-processing optimization**
   - Adjust confidence thresholds
   - Optimize NMS parameters
   - Add rule-based fallback logic
   - Test-Time Augmentation (TTA)

## 5.2 Speed Optimization (Inference Acceleration)

- **Model compression**: knowledge distillation, structured pruning, unstructured pruning
- **Quantization**: FP16 / INT8 / INT4 (evaluate accuracy loss)
- **Inference frameworks**: TensorRT / ONNX Ru...(truncated)

files.contentPreview:
# Model Training Reference / 模型训练参考

> Loaded on demand by `ai-model-lifecycle` SKILL.md when the user is configuring or troubleshooting training.

---

# English

## 4.1 Pre-Training Setup

**Dataset splitting:**
- Standard: Train 70% / Val 15% / Test 15%
- Principles:
  - Stratified sampling by source / time to avoid leakage
  - Roughly consistent class distribution across splits
  - Test set should approximate real production distribution
  - **Once test set is fixed, keep it unchanged across the iteration cycle** (to ensure comparability)

**Training environment:**
- Record full env info: GPU model, CUDA version, framework version, random seed
- Manage all hyperparameters via config files (no hardcoding in source)
- Recommended experiment trackers: MLflow / Weights & Biases / TensorBoard

## 4.2 Training Strategy

**Learning rate:**
- Use warmup + cosine decay or warmup + step decay
- When fine-tuning pretrained models, set backbone LR to ~1/10 of head LR
- If unsure, run a Learning Rate Finder sweep first

**Class imbalance:**
- Data level: oversample minority / undersample majority / SMOTE
- Loss level: Focal Loss / Class-Balanced Loss / Dice Loss (segmentation)
- Sampling level: Class-Balanced Sampling

**Regularization:**
- Dropout / DropPath / Label Smoothing / Weight Decay
- Data augmentation itself is a form of regularization

**Training monitoring:**
- Log per-epoch: train loss, val loss, core metrics
- Watch overfitting signals: val loss rising, train-val gap wid...(truncated)
